what: SmolVLA FP32-ONNX vs TRT-engine precision sweep, run on DGX Spark (GB10/Blackwell, TRT 10.13) as a cheap proxy before the Orin Nano (Ampere, TRT 10.3)
reference: ORT CPU FP32 (true FP32), identical seeded inputs

results.verdict: BROKEN - vision-tower fp16 overflow (inf attn constants)
BROKEN - TRT picks fp16 for the sensitive layers
near-lossless, RECOMMENDED (fp32 dynamic range, tensor-core speed)

key_findings: Pure-TRT build succeeds: NonZero/ScatterND from vision_model masked-indexing are accepted by TRT 10.x (no hard abort).
FP16 is unusable: 730 fp16-overflowing constants incl. literal inf in every vision self_attn layer; cosine collapses to 0.805 (wrong signs).
trtexec --layerPrecisions name-pinning does NOT work here: myelin fusion erases semantic names (Softmax/Norm -> __myl_* supernodes) and the wildcard is default-only, not substring.
BF16 is the fix: same exponent range as FP32 so no overflow; cosine 0.9974.
Latencies are Blackwell/opt-0 only; Orin Nano will be slower. Hitting 10 Hz likely needs fewer denoise steps too, independent of precision.
